div.p: The Monthly Historical Report of the United States Army Group of AMAG, for August, states that the Group “began its actual supply of the Greek armed forces when, on 2 August 1947, the first American ship arrived at Piraeus with a military general cargo, vehicles and rations. Following in the wake of this vessel during August were ten additional American ships with military supplies and equipment.” (868.20 Missions/9–2647) For the Department’s statement of August 22 on early aspects of aid to Greece, see Department of State Bulletin, August 31, 1947, page 428.
